dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for headache call at Richmond.
Hi, my name is Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I'm having a bad headache, but I've been vomiting all morning too.
Leonardo, what developments have occurred since your initial call this morning?
He stayed up all night drinking and could not stop vomiting this morning. He feels very weak and dizzy.
Leonardo, can you tell me what year you were born?
I was born in 1976.
What county are we in now, Leonardo?
Richmond County.
What day is it, as best as you can recall?
I think it's Friday, December 7th.
Leonardo, can you recount the events leading up to calling 911 today?
I've been vomiting at least ten times and feel very shaky. The pain is so bad it's stopping me from standing straight.
Leonardo, we'll check your airway now. I will look for any signs of obstruction or difficulty breathing. Is that okay?
Sure, go ahead.
I found no signs of airway obstruction. It is patent and clear.
Leonardo, can you take a deep breath, like blowing out candles? Let me check your breathing pattern.
Chest rising and falling evenly on both sides. I will now attach a device to your finger for oxygen saturation monitoring.
His oxygen saturation is 100%.
His respiratory rate is 18 and looks regular.
Leonardo, I will check your pulse now. My two fingers will gently rest on your wrists for a moment. Your skin looks warm.
Right now, his pulse is 98 and strong.
His blood pressure is 188 over 119 - this is above normal.
I'll check his blood sugar now. Please allow me to use a small finger prick.
There is no initial reading, so we will repeat the test.
He has pale and dry skin. No unusual color or swelling noted. Leonardo, you mentioned you feel shaky - is the feeling still there?
I'm still shivering and feel very weak and chilled.
You mentioned vomiting ten times, Leonardo. We need to check for signs of blood or complications. Can you tell us anything about the vomit?
Two times there was a bit of blood coming up.
Leonardo, could you share your history of medicines, surgeries, or chronic illnesses with us?
I have high blood pressure and an ulcer removed two years ago.
Did you take any medications at the time of the surgery or for your blood pressure?
Only lisinopril for my high blood pressure.
He began heavy drinking on Sunday and stopped drinking at 10 PM yesterday. He woke up at 7 AM with nausea and dizziness.
We noticed you mentioned GERD earlier. Is this correct?
Yes, the doctor said I have GERD.
Would you mind sharing your age and address?
I'm 43. The address is redacted.
Leonardo, how bad is the headache from 0 to 10, where 10 is the worst pain you've felt?
It's about a 7.
Is it throbbing, burning, or does it spread to another part of your body?
It stays at the back of my head and side, and shifts to my stomach when I lie down.
Leonardo, I'll press gently on your lower abdomen now. You said it is tender - let me locate the source.
It is tender here, in the lower quadrant area.
I will now check his temperature. It reads 98.7°F.
Leonardo, can you lie still while I attach some pads to your chest and arms for an EKG? It helps us check your heart rhythm.
I don't feel anything yet.
The EKG is showing a sinus rhythm with no immediate abnormalities.
Performing a 4-lead cardiac monitoring to get a baseline of his heart function.
The 4-lead monitor is active and showing sinus rhythm.
Taking a 12-lead EKG now for a more comprehensive look.
His 12-lead EKG shows sinus rhythm with no immediate findings.
We found signs of alcohol withdrawal and vomiting. We're following protocol 3-9 for medical transport. We will monitor him carefully during transport.
Let me recheck his vitals now. Starting with his pulse.
His pulse is now at 104.
His blood pressure is now 143 over 99 - still above normal.
No change in respiratory rate - 18 breaths per minute. Oxygen saturation is 95 now.
Blood sugar is now 133. That's within a typical range.
Another 12-lead EKG was done. His heart is still in sinus rhythm.
Leonardo, where would you like to be taken for treatment?
I didn't think about it. Maybe St. Mary's?
St. Mary's is a good choice. We've already called them to prepare for your arrival. We'll transport you there now.
We will now assist you onto the stretcher and make sure you are safely secured.
We'll keep checking on you throughout the ride. If at any time you feel worse, let us know.
All safety lines and harnesses are secure. You are in a stable position currently.
Leonardo, please try to stay relaxed and let us know if you need anything. We are with you for the whole trip.
We are on our way. I will continue to monitor your condition and speak with you as needed.
The ED has been informed about Leonardo's high BP, stable heart rhythm, and EKG result. Protocol 3-9 is in place, and we will continue monitoring.
Hospital ED was pre-notified of Leonardo's condition and our expected arrival. All vitals and tests are recorded.
All transport notes and readings are in the PCR. We will hand those over on arrival.
Leonardo, please try to stay relaxed and let us know if you need anything. We are with you for the whole trip.
We are on our way. I will continue to monitor your condition and speak with you as needed.
All readings are consistent so far. Leonardo remains stable with no new concerns.
We will carefully transfer you from the ambulance stretcher to a hospital bed once we arrive at St. Mary's.
